x-enumeration-literals.name: Recourse Factoring Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description: The business is liable for any unpaid invoices. If a customer fails to pay, the business must either repay the advance or replace the unpaid invoice with another invoice of equivalent value.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Non-Recourse Factoring Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description: The factoring company assumes the risk of non-payment. If the customer defaults, the business is not liable for repaying the advance. However, this option typically comes with higher fees and may only apply in cases of customer insolvency.
